videos.title: South Korea's highest-paid math tutor makes $14 million a year, more than Son Heung-min, their most famous footballer. His name is Hyun Woo-jin. He graduated from Stanford's math department in three years and three months. His textbook series, Neuron, sells over a million copies a year. Every November, South Korea pauses for a single nine-hour exam called the Suneung. The Air Force halts training flights. For 35 minutes during the English listening section, no commercial flights can take off or land at Incheon, one of Asia's busiest airports. About 8,000 police officers escort late students on motorcycles with sirens running. Inside that exam, the most consequential questions are called killer questions. They're deliberately designed to fall outside the public school curriculum. They exist to separate the top one percent from everyone else, because that one percent gets into Seoul National, Korea, and Yonsei. The SKY universities that produce around 30% of Korea's top-1,000-company CEOs. Parents can't evaluate the curriculum themselves. They can't reverse-engineer the killer question. So they borrow Hyun Woo-jin's judgment. If your child went through Neuron and still failed, you did what every reasonable parent in the neighborhood does. The fault is your child's, or fate's. Not yours. That's what's actually being sold. Not math. Certainty. Korean households spent 29.2 trillion won on private tutoring in 2024, an all-time record, even as the school-age population dropped another 1.5%. A country is, in a measurable sense, choosing not to exist in order to keep paying for that certainty.

We once discussed veterinary medicine from the perspective of how humans came to the idea of helping other living creatures while spending their own time and resources on it. Strictly speaking, this mostly applies to pets, because in the industrial world veterinarians often work with farm animals. Today I want you to think about how incredibly complex this science really is. A veterinarian has to understand the anatomy and physiology of dozens of different species in order to help them. From a tiny aquarium fish to a massive python, any creature may need care, and it is honestly impressive how veterinarians manage to keep all of this knowledge in their minds while making decisions that can determine the fate of their patients. Veterinarians save tens of thousands of beloved pets and prevent epidemics among farm animals, which in many ways also means protecting human populations. Thank you, veterinarians. #Veterinarian #Medicine #Animals #edit #Pets

bakery style oreo stuffed cookies 🍪🥛    recipe: ingredients: ¾ cup salted butter, softened (170 g) ¾ cup packed brown sugar (150 g) ¼ cup granulated sugar (50 g) 1 large egg  1 tsp vanilla (5 g) 2 cups all-purpose flour (240 g) 1 tbsp cornstarch (8 g) ½ tsp baking soda (3 g) ¼ tsp salt (1.5 g) oreos, some whole some  roughly chopped  white chocolate (measure with your heart)  & choceur cookie and cream chocolate’s   instructions: cream the butter and sugars together  until smooth and slightly fluffy. mix in the egg and vanilla until fully combined. in a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt. fold the dry ingredients into the wet just until combined.  chop some of the oreos into small chunks ( leave some for topping ) and fold them in along with the white chocolate. scoop the dough into 8 portions, flatten and add an oreo inside and chocolate then shape into tall mounds. ( i flatten the top but keep them tall ) bake at 375°F for 11–14 minutes, until the edges are set and the centers are still soft. ( baking time may vary ) let cool to set the cookies!  i added moser roths white chocolate to the bottom & inside !    #oreo #cookies #chocolatechipcookies #Recipe #baking

yummy #junkfood #processedfood #sugar #slop ever have one of those days where you wake up thinking it’s going to be simple, calm, maybe even boring… and then the universe decides to roll out a full-on spectacle? Not a disaster. Not a tragedy. Just a slow, creeping chaos that nudges your plans off the table until all you can do is shrug and say, “Okay… let’s dance.” It’s like suddenly you’re the lead in a story you didn’t audition for. You were just trying to exist quietly, keep to your lane, maybe sip your coffee in peace. And then—bam—a whirlwind of people, events, and random coincidences crashes in. Everything ordinary turns loud, confusing, and somehow hilarious. There’s always that presence. The one that doesn’t leave, no matter how much you wish they would. Persistent, annoying, impossible to ignore. They challenge your patience, test your limits, make you laugh when you least expect it. And slowly, almost imperceptibly, they start to shift the world around you. Things feel lighter. Moments feel brighter. Walls you built begin to crumble. Then comes the unexpected twist. The one that doesn’t fit neatly into your plan, your comfort zone, your carefully curated life. They see past the surface—the tired, guarded exterior—and into the heart underneath. They don’t soften the chaos, they exist within it, and in doing so, force you to see yourself differently. Vulnerable, messy, real. It’s terrifying. It’s exhilarating. It’s alive. We all have our “safe corners,” spaces where the world feels predictable and quiet. But staying there too long? That’s not living. Growth waits in the interruptions, the detours, the moments that throw your day off balance. You don’t need to have a plan. You just need to keep showing up, keep opening doors, keep letting the story surprise you. Life isn’t linear. It’s a winding path of unexpected encounters, awkward coincidences, and loud, uninvited chaos. Some pass through and leave only echoes. Others stay long enough to shift the story, maybe even change it. And all of it—the frustration, the wonder, the laughter, the heartbreak—is yours. It shapes the narrative that only you can tell. So if today feels like a musical number you didn’t ask for, remember: you don’t need everything neat, perfect, or polished. You just need to move forward, to stumble, to dance a little, to let the interruptions in. Because in that chaos, in the mess, in the unplanned moments… that’s where you find the parts of yourself you didn’t know were waiting. And maybe, somewhere in all of it, you discover the story that’s truly yours—wild, imperfect, messy, and unforgettable. #targetaudience

This routine is for anyone who is not dealing with extreme damage but wants over all glossier & softer hair this is the the exact routine I built for getting glass hair on fine hair but will work for the wavy curly hair girls too!!  the science: the flatten your cuticle lies, the more light it reflects that's what gives the illusion of glass. glossy hair isn't a product, it's the consistency of routine over time that’s keeping that cuticle smooth from prewash to final styling  it took me a while to figure out the system, but once I did, the results compounded and now I wanna share my favourite tips with you because this is the routine that actually for every  wash day. the 5 things I never skip: 1. always create a barrier before shampoo: hair oil or conditioner first. less friction = less cuticle damage = more shine 2. weekly hair mask. deep hydration is what keeps that cuticle flat and the shine consistent. 3. a glossing treatment every 3-4 weeks  this is the step that takes glossy to glass  4. leave-in conditioner every single wash. beats frizz and keeps the cuticle flat between washes. 5. the hair sandwich method oil before heat styling AND after. then always finish with a cool shot to lock the cuticle in place ✨ Hope these haircare tips help you guys!! #hair #haircare #glasshair #hairgloss

Ooey-Gooey Scoopable Nutella Cookie Pie🤤 Yields: 15-16 Chunky Cookies INGREDIENTS:  230g Cold Chopped Unsalted Butter  160g Caster Sugar  160g Light Brown Sugar  450g Chocolate Chunks or chips  475g Plain Flour  1 tsp Salt 3 tsp Baking Powder  3/4 tsp Baking Soda  2 Eggs  1 Egg Yolk  1 tsp Vanilla  INSTRUCTIONS: •Cut cling wrap into small squares. Scoop ½ teaspoon Nutella onto individual cling-wrap squares. Fold to seal and freeze until solid. (Repeat for however many Nutella stuffed cookies you plan to make.) •In a large mixing bowl, add the cold cubed butter and whisk until slightly creamy. Add the white sugar and light brown sugar, then whisk together until well combined and fluffy. •Add the eggs, vanilla extract, and chocolate chips or chunks. Whisk until fully incorporated. •Add the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Gently mix until just combined, do not overmix. •Portion the dough into 90g balls. Press a frozen Nutella piece into the centre of each dough ball, seal well, and roll into a smooth ball. •Place the stuffed cookie dough balls onto a tray and freeze for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for best results). •Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). •Take any shape baking dish circle, square, rectangle anything and line it with parchment paper. Arrange frozen cookie dough balls in it leaving some space between them. •Bake for 10-15 minutes (timing depends on the type of pan) or until the tops are lightly golden while the centres remain soft and gooey. •Remove from the oven and let cool slightly. •Serve warm with ice cream and chocolate sauce enjoy!✨ NOTE: Store any extra dough balls in an airtight container or freezer bag and freeze for later. Whenever a cookie craving hits, bake straight from frozen🫶🏻 #cookie #nutella #cookiepie #fyp #viral

Add the white sugar and light brown sugar, then whisk together until well combined and fluffy. •Add the eggs, vanilla extract, and chocolate chips or chunks. Whisk until fully incorporated. •Add the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Gently mix until just combined, do not overmix. •Portion the dough into 75-80g balls. •Place the cookie dough balls onto a tray and freeze for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for best results). •Preheat oven to 165-170C. Bake for 10-15 minutes or until the tops are lightly golden while the centres remain soft. •Remove from the oven and let cool slightly. •Serve warm with ice cream. NOTE: Store any extra dough balls in an airtight container or freezer bag and freeze for later. #mascara                                    Un mascara est un produit de maquillage destiné à être appliqué sur les cils afin de les allonger, les épaissir, les recourber et intensifier le regard. Il se présente généralement sous forme liquide ou crémeuse, contenu dans un tube avec une brosse applicatrice. Le mascara permet de donner plus de volume et de définition aux cils en les colorant, le plus souvent en noir, mais aussi en brun ou en couleurs plus originales. Selon sa formule et la forme de la brosse, il peut offrir différents effets : naturel, volumineux, allongeant ou recourbant. Facile à utiliser, il est un élément essentiel du maquillage des yeux, mais reste temporaire et s’enlève avec un démaquillant adapté.                     #lashes #prt #fyp #foryoupagе
